title: Full-Wave Bridge Rectifier Circuit
description: Converts both half-cycles of AC to DC using four diodes in a bridge configuration. Doubles ripple frequency and significantly improves DC output quality.
theory: A full-wave bridge rectifier uses four diodes arranged in a bridge (Graetz bridge) configuration to convert both positive and negative half-cycles of AC input into a unidirectional DC output. During the positive half-cycle of AC input, diodes D1 and D2 are forward-biased and conduct, while D3 and D4 are reverse-biased and block. During the negative half-cycle, D3 and D4 conduct while D1 and D2 block. In both cases, current flows through the load resistor R_L in the same direction, producing a full-wave rectified output. The average DC output voltage is 0.636 × Vm (compared to 0.318 × Vm for half-wave), and since both half-cycles are utilized, the ripple frequency is doubled (2f) relative to the supply frequency. The rectification efficiency is approximately 81.2% — double that of the half-wave rectifier. No center-tapped transformer is needed, making it practical and widely used in AC/DC power supplies. Each diode in the conducting pair drops approximately 0.7V (silicon), so the actual output is Vm − 1.4V.
keyPoints: Uses 4 diodes in a Graetz bridge — BOTH half-cycles contribute to DC output.
Positive half-cycle: D1 and D2 conduct; Negative half-cycle: D3 and D4 conduct.
Average DC output voltage = 0.636 × Vm (twice that of half-wave rectifier).
Ripple frequency = 2f (e.g., 100 Hz for a 50 Hz supply) — easier to filter.
No center-tapped transformer required — works with a standard transformer.
Each conducting pair of diodes drops ~1.4 V total (2 × 0.7 V silicon drops).
PIV per diode = Vm (lower than center-tap full-wave rectifier's 2Vm).
examTip: Bridge rectifier: BOTH half-cycles contribute. Positive cycle: D1,D2 conduct. Negative cycle: D3,D4 conduct. DC output = 0.636 × Vm. Efficiency = 81.2%. Ripple factor = 0.482. Ripple frequency = 2f (100Hz for 50Hz supply). No center-tap transformer needed. Each diode drops ~0.7V → output = Vm - 1.4V.
useCases: Standard AC-to-DC power adapters (phone chargers, laptop adapters).
Industrial power supplies requiring high efficiency.
Battery charging systems with good DC quality.
Welding power supplies and motor drive circuits.

wires.comment: AC source top terminal to bridge left-top
Output+ rail down to RL top
Join RL bottom and C1 bottom
AC source bottom terminal down to bridge left-bottom
Left-bottom rail to D3 anode (reversed = cathode side)
D4 cathode to output bottom rail

junctions.comment: Top bridge junction between D1 and D2
Bottom bridge junction between D3 and D4
